basic_info.description: I'm Lucy Gelderloos, and I'm a full-stack software developer. I spent the last ten-plus years working in a variety of staff positions in law firms, starting in the copy room and ending up in IT support. I had the opportunity to learn skills ranging from unjamming copiers to creating PowerPoint templates to proofreading complex legal documents to maintaining the firm's intranet. I also received a Master's degree in Environmental Studies from The Evergreen State College, where along with refining my research and writing skills, I learned the basics of visualization and analysis tools R and ArcGIS. In both work and school, I've found that I'm most satisfied when I'm learning technical skills and using those skills to solve problems. I particularly enjoyed working with HTML and CSS, and continually sought out projects that would let me use those skills. Now I'm taking the leap and hoping to find a job where coding can be the main focus. Ideally, I'd like to combine this with my education and work on fighting climate change, either through state or local government or in the private sector.

projects.title: Horned Beasts
projects.description: Users can view images of horned animals, filter by number of horns, and vote for their favorites.

projects.title: Cloud Ten
projects.description: An uplifing app to track your learning and practice flash cards.

projects.title: Odd Duck Product Survey
projects.description: Users can vote on products the company should produce; the voting data is compiled and displayed on a results page.

projects.title: Salmon Cookies
projects.description: A home page for Pat's Samlon Cookies, with a page to estimate sales and staffing.
